model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=242 dy=-318 conf=1.00 -> (426,108)
  iter 1: at (426,108) said dx=-40 dy=170 conf=1.00 -> (386,278)
  iter 2: at (406,193) said dx=66 dy=8 conf=1.00 -> (472,201)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the "Beta" project card.
The red crosshair is currently at (422, 195) as indicated in the bottom right corner of the image (though the prompt says "Previous iterations: ... -> (472, 201)"). Let's look at the image.
The red crosshair is a red plus sign. It is located on the Beta project card.
The center of the Beta project card seems to be roughly where the crosshair is.
Let's estimate the center of the Beta card.
The Beta card is the middle one in the top row.
Its top-left corner is around (270, 185) and bottom-right is around (505, 375).
Wait, the image coordinate system usually has (0,0) at the top-left.
The crosshair is at (422, 195) based on the text in the bottom right.
The Beta card is the one with "Beta" written on it.
The crosshair is positioned over the Beta card, slightly to the right and above the center of the card itself.
The center of the Beta card seems to be around x=387, y=280.
Wait, looking at the image, the red crosshair is at (422, 195).
The Beta card spans from roughly x=270 to x=505 and y=185 to y=375.
The center would be roughly x=(270+505)/2 = 387.5, y=(185+375)/2 = 280.
Current position: (422, 195).
Target: (387, 280).
dx = 387 - 422 = -35.
dy = 280 - 195 = 85.

Let me re-examine the red crosshair. It's a red plus sign inside a blue circle.
Actually, the red crosshair is the red plus sign. The blue circle is the spatial reference.
Wait, the instructions say: "A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position... A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference."
In the image, there is a red plus sign inside a blue circle.
Actually, the red plus sign IS the crosshair. The blue circle is the reference.
The crosshair is at (422, 195).
The center of the Beta card:
Left edge: ~270
Right edge: ~505
Top edge: ~185
Bottom edge: ~375
Center: ~ (387, 280)
Offset from (422, 195) to (387, 280):
dx = 387 - 422 = -35
dy = 280 - 195 = 85

Let's double check the crosshair position.
The crosshair is centered at (422, 195).
The Beta card's text "Beta" is around (300, 300).
The icon is around (305, 240).
The center of the card is roughly (387, 280).
dx = 387 - 422 = -35
dy = 280 - 195 = 85

Wait, let's look at the image again.
The crosshair is actually quite far up.
The Beta card's top border is at about y=185.
The crosshair is at (422, 195). That's very close to the top border.
The center of the card is much lower.
The height of the card is 375 - 185 = 190. Half is 95.
y_center = 185 + 95 = 280.
The width of the card is 505 - 270 = 235. Half is 117.5.
x_center = 270 + 117.5 = 387.5.
Current: (422, 195).
Target: (387, 280).
dx = 387 - 422 = -35.
dy = 280 - 195 = 85.

Let me re-read the prompt's previous iterations.
iter 0: (184,426) -> (426,108)
iter 1: (426,108) -> (386,278)
iter 2: (406,193) -> (472,201)
Now we are at (422, 195).
Wait, if the previous iter 2 ended at (472, 201), and the current image says (422, 195)... maybe the coordinates in the bottom right are the current crosshair.
Yes, (422, 195) is where the crosshair is.
The target is the Beta project card center.
Beta card center is approx (387, 280).
dx = 387 - 422 = -35.
dy = 280 - 195 = 85.
Actually, looking at the image, the crosshair is a bit to the right of center.
Center of Beta card is roughly where the "Beta" text starts horizontally, shifted right.
Let's try to be more precise.
Beta card boundaries:
Left: x=271
Right: x=503
Top: y=185
Bottom: y=375
Center x = (271+503)/2 = 387
Center y = (185+375)/2 = 280
Current crosshair (422, 195).
dx = 387 - 422 = -35
dy = 280 - 195 = 85
Confidence: 1.0.
